# Wave Client

> Wave Client is a modern, platform-agnostic REST API client that runs both as a Visual Studio Code extension and as a standalone web app. It builds and sends HTTP, WebSocket, and SSE requests; organizes them into collections; parameterizes them with environments and variables; validates responses; chains them into flows and test suites; and includes a built-in AI assistant. This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org) to help language models discover and navigate the documentation. All links are relative to this file's location (the `docs/` directory) and resolve on GitHub.

## Getting started

- [Installation](getting-started/installation.md): Prerequisites and setup for the VS Code extension and the web app.
- [Quick Start](getting-started/quick-start.md): Send your first request, step by step.

## Features

- [Requests](features/requests.md): HTTP, WebSocket, and SSE requests; URL, params, headers, body types, the response viewer, the "Sent" view, request cancellation, response download, and keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl/Cmd+S save).
- [Collections](features/collections.md): Organizing requests into nested folders; import (Postman, OpenAPI/Swagger, full `.http` syntax) with content-based format auto-detection, into a new or existing collection, with optional find/replace rules; export; running collections; new folders; move (requests and whole folders, atomic) and duplicate.
- [Environments](features/environments.md): Environment variables, global vs. environment scope, selection, and import (Wave or Postman environment JSON) with format auto-detection.
- [Variables](features/variables.md): `{{variable}}` resolution, dynamic `_fn_` functions (UUIDs, random data, dates), and temporary per-request placeholder overrides.
- [Auth](features/auth.md): Authentication types (API Key, Basic, Digest, OAuth2 Refresh/Client Credentials/Authorization Code with PKCE, and HMAC) and where they apply.
- [Validations](features/validations.md): Response validation rules, operators, JSONPath, and JSON Schema.
- [Wave Store](features/wave-store.md): Reusable cookies, auth credentials, proxies, and client certificates.
- [Flows](features/flows.md): Chaining requests on a canvas, conditional connectors, and passing data via aliases/JSONPath.
- [Test Lab](features/tests.md): Building and running test suites with assertions, data-driven test cases, and structured per-case overrides (variables, headers, params, body, auth, validation).
- [Reporting](features/reporting.md): Interactive HTML run reports for collections, flows, and test suites, with the on-wire "Sent" request, friendly transport errors, and validation-aware pass/fail classification.
- [Settings](features/settings.md): Data storage, encryption, and AI settings.
- [AI & Wave Arena](features/ai-arena.md): The in-app AI assistant, its commands, and the MCP server for external AI tools.

## Platforms

- [VS Code extension](platforms/vscode.md): Opening Wave Client (command and `Ctrl+Alt+W` / `Cmd+Alt+W`), data storage, networking, and theme.
- [Web app](platforms/web-app.md): Running the local server + UI, default ports (3456/5173), connection status, and troubleshooting.

## Reference

- [Design & Architecture](design.md): Monorepo and package responsibilities, the adapter pattern, services, the Zustand store, hooks, and the Result pattern.
- [Wave Schemas](schemas.md): The persisted collection and environment file formats (versioned Zod schemas), field by field, with validation boundaries and version history.
- [Test Suite Schema Reference](test-suite-schema.md): The persisted test-suite/test-case file format, the merge-vs-replace override semantics, validation precedence, and authoring tips for developers and AI agents.
- [Versioning](versioning.md): The four independent version tracks (VS Code app, web app, core platform, Wave schemas), semver semantics per track, and the manual bump checklists.
- [Build Your Own Client](build-your-own-client.md): How to build a new Wave Client client (CLI, desktop, etc.) on the platform-agnostic core by implementing the IPlatformAdapter interface; covers UI and headless clients.
- [Release Notes](release-notes.md): Capabilities by area for the public beta.

## Optional

- [Documentation index](README.md): The human-facing landing page and table of contents.
- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): The full text of every documentation page concatenated into a single file for direct ingestion.
- [Dynamic function reference](../packages/core/src/utils/functions/README.md): The authoritative catalog of `_fn_` functions, maintained alongside the code.
- [MCP server reference](../packages/mcp-server/README.md): Tools exposed to external AI agents.
